Free Text: A Planned Crossing Point (PCP) is a risk-assessed location that allows an Individual Working Alone (IWA) or a Controller of Site Safety (COSS) with up to three others to cross no more than two open lines without contacting the signaller. PCPs can only be used as part of a planned Safe System of Work detailed in the Safe Work Pack (SWP), never for ad hoc use, and not during darkness or poor visibility. Before crossing, confirm you are at the correct PCP, check sighting distances, and ensure no trains are approaching; each person must look for trains while crossing together in a direct route without stopping. PCPs  are listed in RailHub and ELLIPSE; report any obstructions and do not use if conditions or maintenance requirements are compromised. Do Not use if it feels unsafe to do so.

Free Text: Risk Rating 6, Hazard Rating 2,Score 12    The RRV gates are accessed via a tarmac pad owned by KCC Highways who require a permit to be granted by them before use of the access. The permit should be applied for 6 weeks prior to use. Permit not required for foot access. A permit is required for any setting down or unloading on the pad & for working on, storing any materials on or parking or traversing using a vehicle to get to the gates. The restriction also prohibits the removal for any reason of the installed bollards and the crossing of the footpath. If not adhered to, Highways have the power to prosecute NWR and stop any works and block the gates from all future use. Please contact the local MPC for more details.

Free Text: This applies to anyone accessing ACR via Rough or Crickets Crossings. Access through the farm and over their private property to the Xing?s is only granted with either green magnetic stickers attached to every van in attendance <available from the local MPC> or with advanced notice and agreement with the Farm Owner / Farm Manager. Any unauthorised vehicle on site will be asked to leave. Vehicles must obey the 10mph farm wide speed limit at all times. NR vans must give way to all farm vehicles, equipment and people as they have right of way. No roads, tracks or storage units will be obstructed at any time. The farm car park is not to be used by Network Rail staff or vehicles. No litter or food stuff is to be left on site at any time. Welfare facilities must be provided and anyone caught urinating or defecating on site will be banned for life and arrested. Mirrors in place are for safety of all traffice and must be used. Access is limited to 2 vehicles per day.
